author: Janet Rodriguez
content: Member called regarding denied MRI claim CLM-MB-003. Member states his PCP referred him to orthopedics and the MRI was ordered by the orthopedic specialist. Member was not informed that prior authorization was required. Explained the prior auth requirement under his PPO plan for advanced imaging. Member is upset — requesting appeal. Initiated appeal process and provided case reference number. Advised member that appeal decision typically takes 30 days.

author: Janet Rodriguez
content: Appeal for MRI claim CLM-MB-003 has been approved. Medical necessity confirmed by clinical review team. Retroactive prior authorization AUTH-MB-001 issued. Original claim reprocessed as CLM-MB-004 with standard in-network benefits applied. Member notified by mail. Case closed.

author: Diana Vargas
content: Member called regarding denied prior authorization AUTH-MC-001 for Ozempic. Member states her endocrinologist (Dr. Reyes) recommended switching from Metformin to Ozempic due to A1C of 7.8% and inadequate glucose control. Explained step therapy policy: plan requires trial of preferred SGLT2 inhibitor (Jardiance) before GLP-1 agonist coverage. Member expressed frustration — feels the plan is overriding her doctor's clinical judgment. Advised member that her endocrinologist can prescribe Jardiance and if it is not effective or causes side effects, a new Ozempic authorization can be submitted with clinical documentation of treatment failure. Member acknowledged but remains dissatisfied.

author: Diana Vargas
content: Annual wellness visit completed. Member's care plan updated. A1C now at 6.9% (at target) since switching to Ozempic in July 2025. Blood pressure well-controlled on Lisinopril 20mg. Diabetic retinopathy screening completed October 2025 — no retinopathy detected. Member expressed satisfaction with current treatment regimen. Next A1C check scheduled for March 2026. Ozempic prior auth valid through July 2026. No changes to care plan needed at this time.

author: Marcus Williams
content: Member called regarding denied anesthesia claim CLM-SD-014 ($4,200). Member gave birth at University Hospital on 10/5/2025 and received epidural anesthesia during labor. The anesthesiologist (Dr. Kowalski) was assigned by the hospital — member had no choice or advance notice that the provider was out-of-network. Member is extremely upset about receiving a $4,200 balance bill. Explained that the claim was denied because the anesthesiologist is not in our Blue Access network. Member referenced the No Surprises Act, which protects patients from surprise out-of-network billing at in-network facilities. Initiated appeal under surprise billing protections. Advised member that this type of appeal is typically resolved in the patient's favor. Member remains very frustrated but appreciates the escalation. Case flagged as high priority.

author: Marcus Williams
content: Appeal for anesthesia claim CLM-SD-014 approved. No Surprises Act applies — patient received emergency/ancillary services from an out-of-network provider at an in-network facility without ability to choose an in-network alternative. Original denied claim voided. New claim CLM-SD-015 processed using qualifying payment amount (in-network median rate). Member responsibility reduced from $4,200 to $308 (standard coinsurance after deductible). Difference of $3,892 paid by plan. Member notified by mail and secure message. Case closed.

author: Robert Moore
content: Member called regarding ER facility claim CLM-MA-006 from 5/14 visit to NYU Langone ED. Member presented with hypertensive crisis (BP 210/125), received CT head and IV labetalol, and was discharged. Member received EOB showing $3,322 member responsibility and is concerned about the cost. Explained that the Silver HMO plan has a $3,000 individual deductible for facility services, which was mostly consumed by this ER visit. Member had only $92 of deductible used prior to the ER visit. Remaining balance is 10% coinsurance. Member asked about payment plan options — transferred to billing department. Follow-up needed to confirm billing arrangements.

author: Sarah Martinez
content: Care management enrollment outreach call completed. Member has multiple chronic conditions (Type 2 Diabetes, Hypertension) with recent hypertensive emergency (May 2025) and suboptimal glycemic control (A1C 8.5% as of June). Member recently started Ozempic (GLP-1) after Jardiance was insufficient. Discussed care management program benefits: regular check-ins, medication adherence support, care coordination between PCP (Dr. Clark), endocrinologist (Dr. Patel), and cardiologist (Dr. F. Clark). Member agreed to enroll. Initial care plan established: monthly phone check-ins, quarterly A1C monitoring, blood pressure log review. Next check-in scheduled for September.

author: Sarah Martinez
content: Care management progress note. Member's October PCP visit shows significant improvement: A1C dropped from 8.5% to 7.2% since starting Ozempic in August. Blood pressure well-controlled at 132/82 on triple therapy (Amlodipine 10mg, Lisinopril 10mg, ongoing monitoring). Member reports improved energy and compliance with medication regimen. Ozempic titrated to 1mg maintenance dose in September without GI side effects. Cardiology follow-up from June showed mild LVH but no structural concerns — annual echo recommended. Member engaged and motivated. Care plan updated: continue monthly calls, next A1C at December wellness visit. Target: A1C <7.0% by year-end.

author: Lisa Martinez
content: Member called regarding COBRA enrollment following involuntary termination from Meridian Healthcare Systems effective 4/30/2025. Member was laid off and did not initially elect COBRA. Member is 20 weeks pregnant and has a scheduled anatomy scan. Discovered she is currently uninsured. Explained COBRA election rights: member has 60 days from qualifying event (4/30) to elect COBRA, with retroactive coverage to 5/1/2025. COBRA premium is $663.00/month (full premium + 2% admin fee) for Gold PPO plan. Member elected COBRA retroactive to 5/1/2025. First premium payment (May + June) of $1,326.00 due within 45 days. Claims from 5/1 forward will be covered once premium received. Member relieved to have coverage restored for prenatal care. Note: member indicated financial concern about ongoing COBRA premiums — may only be able to afford May and June while job searching.

author: Nancy Jackson
content: Member called to add newborn dependent to current Pinnacle Financial Group Silver PPO plan (PLN-FCADA8BD). Baby Isabella Rivera born 10/1/2025 at Cedars-Sinai Medical Center. Qualifying life event — birth of child. Member has 30 days from birth to add dependent. Coverage effective retroactive to date of birth (10/1/2025). Member provided baby's SSN for enrollment. Coverage type updated from Individual to Family. Dependent enrollment processed. Member also asked about well-baby visit coverage under new plan — advised that preventive pediatric visits are covered at 100% with no cost-sharing per ACA requirements.
